<!-- GENERATED by scripts/generate-docs.ts from _pages/. Do not edit this file directly. -->

# Mint

The token holds no sale logic. Every mint goes through an authorized minter,
and the common case is the [FixedPriceMinter](/docs/surface/contracts/fixed-price-minter)
clone the factory wires to a sequential collection at creation. You mint by
calling the **minter**, not the collection; the minter takes payment and calls
the collection's non-payable `mintTo` to issue the tokens.

Find a collection's minter address from the `SurfaceCreated` event the factory
emitted at deploy (its `primaryMinter` field), or from the collection-to-minter
binding your indexer recorded. Collections deployed with a custom minter
(`createSurfaceCustom`, or any pooled collection) expose whatever mint ABI
that minter defines; the rest of this guide is the canonical minter.

```solidity
function mint(address to, uint256 quantity, address referrer, bytes calldata data) external payable;
function priceOf(address to, uint256 quantity) external view returns (uint256);
```

- `to` is the recipient and the address any gate evaluates (an allowlist gates
  the collector, not the payer); `msg.sender` is the payer, so to mint for
  another address pass it as `to`
- `referrer` receives the referral share (`referralShareBps`, initialized to
  its 10% cap) out of the price when nonzero. `address(0)` directs the full
  price to the payout
- `data` carries caller-supplied input a gate consumes, an ABI-encoded
  `bytes32[]` Merkle proof for the allowlist. Gates that read only chain state
  (the per-wallet cap) need no `data`; pass `0x`

## Price resolution

If the minter has no price strategy set (`priceStrategy() == address(0)`), the
price is `price() * quantity` and payment must match exactly: any other
`msg.value` reverts `WrongPayment`.

If a price strategy is set, the minter calls `priceOf(collection, to, quantity)`
on it once and requires `msg.value >= required`; underpayment reverts
`Underpayment`. Overpayment does not revert. It accrues to the payer as a pull
refund, claimable via `withdraw(msg.sender)`, since a strategy's quote can move
between when a collector reads it and when their transaction lands (for example
a basefee-denominated price).

Read the resolved price ahead of a mint with the minter's `priceOf`:

```bash
cast call <MINTER_ADDRESS> "priceOf(address,uint256)(uint256)" \
  0xYourAddress 1 \
  --rpc-url https://ethereum-rpc.publicnode.com
```

## Sale window

`mintStart` / `mintEnd` (unix seconds, both optional) live on the minter and
gate `mint`:

- Before `mintStart`: reverts `MintNotStarted`
- At or after `mintEnd` (when set): reverts `MintEnded`

The token carries no mint window and no lifecycle status. Anything wanting the
sale phase reads the minter's `mintStart`/`mintEnd`.

## Gates

Two optional gates on the canonical minter, AND-composed in the same call, both
evaluating the recipient `to`:

- **Merkle allowlist** (`allowlistRoot` nonzero): pass an ABI-encoded
  `bytes32[]` proof in `data`. The leaf is the OpenZeppelin standard-merkle-tree
  single-address leaf, `keccak256(bytes.concat(keccak256(abi.encode(to))))`. A
  missing or wrong proof reverts `NotAllowlisted`
- **Per-wallet cap** (`walletCap` nonzero): `mintedBy[to]` plus the requested
  quantity must not exceed the cap, or the call reverts `WalletCapExceeded`. The
  counter increments after a successful mint

Both are live config: setting the root and later clearing it is how a
presale-then-public sale runs on one minter. `maxMints` is the minter's own
sale ceiling across all buyers (`MaxMintsExceeded`), separate from the token's
`supplyCap`.

## Supply cap

The token enforces the supply cap on `mintTo`: `ExceedsCap` reverts when a mint
would push past `supplyCap` (0 means uncapped). Sequential collections bound
mints ever. The cap binds every minter, so no grant can exceed it, and
`lockSupply` freezes it permanently.

## cast example

```bash
cast send <MINTER_ADDRESS> "mint(address,uint256,address,bytes)" \
  0xYourAddress 1 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 0x \
  --value 0.02ether \
  --rpc-url https://ethereum-rpc.publicnode.com \
  --private-key $PRIVATE_KEY
```

With a referrer, pass their address in the third argument.

## viem example

```ts
import {createWalletClient, createPublicClient, http} from 'viem';
import {mainnet} from 'viem/chains';
import {fixedPriceMinterAbi} from '@pin/abi';

const MINTER = '<MINTER_ADDRESS>';

const publicClient = createPublicClient({
  chain: mainnet,
  transport: http('https://ethereum-rpc.publicnode.com'),
});
const walletClient = createWalletClient({
  chain: mainnet,
  transport: http('https://ethereum-rpc.publicnode.com'),
});

const price = await publicClient.readContract({
  address: MINTER,
  abi: fixedPriceMinterAbi,
  functionName: 'priceOf',
  args: [walletClient.account.address, 1n],
});

const hash = await walletClient.writeContract({
  address: MINTER,
  abi: fixedPriceMinterAbi,
  functionName: 'mint',
  args: [walletClient.account.address, 1n, referrerAddress, '0x'],
  value: price,
});

await publicClient.waitForTransactionReceipt({hash});
```

## Reverts to handle

| Error | Where | Cause |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `ZeroQuantity` | minter | `quantity == 0` |
| `MintNotStarted` | minter | before `mintStart` |
| `MintEnded` | minter | at or after `mintEnd` |
| `MaxMintsExceeded` | minter | mint would exceed the minter's `maxMints` |
| `NotAllowlisted` | minter | allowlist set and the proof does not verify for `to` |
| `WalletCapExceeded` | minter | mint would exceed `walletCap` for `to` |
| `WrongPayment` | minter | fixed-price mint, `msg.value != price * quantity` |
| `Underpayment` | minter | strategy-priced mint, `msg.value < quote` |
| `ExceedsCap` | token | mint would exceed the collection's `supplyCap` |

After a successful mint, claim any accrued balance (artist proceeds, referral
share, or your own overpayment refund) with `withdraw(account)` on the minter,
a permissionless trigger that always pays the owed address, never the caller:

```bash
cast send <MINTER_ADDRESS> "withdraw(address)" 0xPayeeAddress \
  --rpc-url https://ethereum-rpc.publicnode.com --private-key $PRIVATE_KEY
```

See [Write a minter](/docs/surface/guides/write-a-minter) for pooled
collections and any collection whose economics (backing, dynamic settlement, id
draws) need a custom minter, and
[FixedPriceMinter](/docs/surface/contracts/fixed-price-minter) for the full
generated reference.
